metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_5091
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4: reverse-IPs and mutated-domain-controls showed l-afadin can interact with alphaE-catenin and alphaN-catenin.

Figure 2, 3, 5: dendritic puncta for N-cadherin and alphaN-catenin in cultured hippocampal neurons were reduced in afadin (AFDN) deficient mice and could be rescued by overexpressing l-afadin (long isoform) but not s-afadin (short isoform). Overexpressing a mutated construct of l-afadin showed its FAB domain was required (Fig. 3). Disrupting the αN-catenin to l-afadin binding incurs the same phenotype (Fig. 5).. Experimental description: Results, Figure 3 legend: "The neurons were obtained from the afadin-deficient mice and were transfected with the indicated constructs"
